data.title: Marker Therapeutics sells 5.03M shares at $3.20 in private placement
data.description:
• Marker Therapeutics (NASDAQ:MRKR) has entered into a securities purchase agreement for a private placement, resulting in gross proceeds of $16.1M.
  • Proceeds from the financing will, among other uses, support the clinical advancement of the Company’s Phase 1 APOLLO study investigating MT-601, a multi-antigen recognizing T cell product, in patients with lymphoma who have relapsed after anti-CD19 chimeric antigen receptor-T cell therapy or where anti-CD19 CAR-T cells are not an option.
  • Marker is selling 5.03M shares of its common stock and accompanying warrants to purchase up to 5.03M shares of common stock, at a purchase price of $3.20/share and accompanying warrant.
  • The pre-funded warrants will be exercisable at a price of $0.001/share, and the accompanying warrants will have an exercise price of $4.03/share.

data.title: Apple's Vision Pro shipments behind Meta, Sony AR/VR devices but quickly reshaping the segment - report
data.description:
Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) headset Vision Pro is reshaping the virtual/mixed reality landscape, and has quickly raced to the third spot in the VR/MR market in 2024, according to research firm TrendForce.

Meta Platforms (META) retained its position as the global leader in VR and MR device shipments, followed by Sony (SONY) and Apple, the report added.

Global shipments of VR and MR headsets are expected to reach about 9.6 million units, growing 8.8% year-over-year in 2024, according to the firm.

This year's shipment performance highlighted three industry trends shaping the global VR and MR ecosystem, which were — dominance of low-cost devices, shift from entertainment accessories to productivity tools, and OLEDoS emerging as the preferred display technology for high-end near-eye devices.

Meta had a 73% market share in 2024, mainly led by the growth of the affordable Quest 3S — priced at $299 — contributing to an 11% year-over-year increase in shipments.

The firm noted that Meta, facing a market constrained by limited new applications for VR and MR, has adopted a strategy of prioritizing affordable pricing to gain consumer interest. Meta has also opted to release the Quest 3S early while also suspending plans for the high-end Quest Pro 2, indicating a shift in focus toward budget-friendly devices, the report added.

Sony's PS VR2 took the second-largest market share at 9% for 2024. Despite the Japanese company's effort to integrate the PS VR2 with personal computers, or PC, platforms using adapters to broaden the content ecosystem, limited functionality and application support have hindered its performance. As a result, annual shipments fell 25% year-over-year, according to the report.

Apple's Vision Pro took a 5% market share, becoming the third-largest player in the VR/MR market. However, sales fell short of previous Apple product launches, mainly due to its high price and limited application resources, the report noted.

Vision Pro's entry, along with Meta's efforts for a budget-friendly model, shows that affordable pricing remains a vital factor for consumer adoption. Price sensitivity among consumers has solidified LCD technology as the dominant display technology for VR/MR devices, with a market share exceeding 80%, the report added.

Vision Pro's launch has shifted VR and MR devices beyond their traditional focus as consumer-oriented entertainment to larger multi-functional tools, with use cases such as document editing, virtual meetings, and applications in healthcare and education, the report noted.

This shift is expected to encourage other brands to reevaluate functional uses of their own VR/MR devices, expanding their role in consumer and enterprise markets, the report stated.

Vision Pro is also the first device in the industry to feature OLEDoS display technology, which has set a new benchmark for product specifications and visual quality in the sector, according to TrendForce.

Apple is expected to launch its next generation of VR/MR headsets as early as 2026, with a strategy to bring two different models aimed at both high-end and mainstream segments.

The high-end version is expected to continue using OLEDoS display technology with a resolution of more than 3,000 pixels per inch, or PPI, for better visual experience. Apple could consider sourcing components from suppliers besides Sony to cut production costs, potentially including Chinese suppliers currently boosting production capacity, the report added.

For its mainstream model, Apple could focus on affordability and cost-efficiency, targeting price-sensitive consumers. Display options for this model could include glass-based Organic Light Emitting Diode, or OLED, displays and Liquid Crystal Display, or LCDs, with Low-Temperature Polycrystalline Oxide, or LTPO, backplane technology, the report noted.

data.title: GFL narrows list of environmental unit to three buyers - CTFN
data.description:
GFL Environmental (NYSE:GFL) has narrowed down the list of potential buyers for it environmental unit to three bidders.

Apollo Global (APO), EQT Partners and Stonepeak remain in the sales process, according to a CTFN report on Thursday, which cited sources close to the matter. The business may fetch about $8 billion in a transaction.

The latest update comes after the Globe and Mail on Wednesday reported that GFL was in exclusive talks with Apollo for an $8 billion deal. Apollo (APO) has emerged as the likely buyer for the division after GFL started a sales process earlier this year.

GFL (GFL) last month said it received several expressions of interest from potential buyers at valuations that should net a minimum of $6 billion in after tax proceeds.

Bloomberg reported earlier this month that Apollo (APO), EQT AB and Stonepeak were among suitors trying to purchase the company's environmental services business.

data.title: Despite Fed's hawkish tone, Wells Fargo forecasts 'solid growth in 2025'
data.description:
While Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve might have put an end to the recent market rally, there is still a noticeable upside for the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) in 2025, according to Wells Fargo equity analyst Christopher Harvey.

“Despite the Fed pivot we see solid growth in 2025, an improving regulatory environment, and good fundamentals,” Harvey stated on Thursday in an investor note.

Moreover, Harvey noted that the monetary policy committee’s rate decision and rhetoric did not change Wells Fargo’s S&P 500 year-end targets of 5,830 and 7,007 for 2024 and 2025, respectively. The index's last close was at 5,872.16 points.

As for how investors should attack the market, Harvey added that he believes in a sector barbell approach with a 40% emphasis on communication services, another 40% towards banks, and the remaining 20% focused on consumer staples.

For investors interested in these market areas, see a grouping of ETFs that focus on each of the above-mentioned areas:

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL).

Banking ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (KBWB), (KBE), (KRE), (KBWR), (IAT), and (FTXO).

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS).

Furthermore, for a broader market approach, here are some exchange-traded funds and mutual funds that can serve as a proxy investment: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (SSO), (RSP), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), and (VFFSX).

data.title: Carnival’s passenger booking trend, guidance in focus during Q4 earnings
data.description:
Carnival (NYSE:CCL) is set to report the fourth quarter results on Friday, and investors will keep an eye on the company’s FY25 initial guidance and passenger booking trends.

Wall Street expects the cruise operator to post EPS of $0.07, while revenue is expected to increase nearly 10% to $5.93B during the quarter.

Cruise line operators expect a record-breaking number of global passengers in 2025 as some forecasts indicate a 20% increase in cruise line passengers. Major cruise lines are already reporting robust advance bookings for 2025.

“At this point in time, 2025 is a historical high on both occupancy and price. Every brand in our portfolio is well booked at higher pricing in 2025,” the company said in its Q3 earnings call, adding that it is expecting 688 dry dock days in 2025, a 17% increase versus 2024, which will impact our overall Y/Y cost comparison by about 0.75.

Seeking Alpha analysts, Wall Street and Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings are bullish and consider the stock Buy and above.

"We get the sense booking patterns remain healthy, and we believe CCL hasn’t witnessed any deterioration in their onboard customer spending patterns," said Stifel analyst Steven Wieczynski.

Over the last two years, CCL has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 88% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen 11 upward revisions and five downward moves. Revenue estimates have seen 16 upward revisions versus one downward move.

Carnival stock has gained nearly 36% so far this year, outperforming the broader S&P500 Index’s gain of over 23%.

data.title: TD Bank raised to Outperform at BMO as risks shift to upside
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets upgraded TD Bank (NYSE:TD) to Outperform from Market Perform as the valuation of the stock appears to be heavily discounted as the bank faces stricter scrutiny from U.S. regulators following failures in its anti-money laundering controls.

Year-to-date, Toronto-Dominion Bank (NYSE:TD) shares have dropped 16%, compared with a 31% increase for the KBW Nasdaq Bank Index (BKX).

"We may be a bit early with the bank in the interregnum but find the 3-to-1 upside/downside skew compelling enough to upgrade TD to Outperform, and collect an attractive dividend yield even if some patience may be required," BMO analyst Sohrab Movahedi wrote in a note to clients.

Recall that earlier this month, the company suspended its medium-term financial targets as it launched a strategic review of its opportunities and priorities.

"Our overarching assumption is that TD’s U.S. Retail segment is not an impaired asset (even if its earnings trajectory is down before up) and the rest of the bank will deliver peer-like performance notwithstanding the distractions associated with this review and leadership changes," Movahedi added.

The Outperform rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating and the average SA Analyst rating, both at Hold, and comes into line with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

TD Bank (NYSE:TD) stock edged up 0.1% in late Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Novartis to close MorphoSys sites in Boston, Munich: report
data.description:
Novartis (NYSE:NVS) is reportedly planning to close MorphoSys’s sites in Boston and Munich, Germany, a move that is expected to impact around 300 workers.

The sites are slated to be closed by the end of 2025. Novartis (NYSE:NVS) made a decision to close the sites based on its evolving R&D operations and an organizational evaluation, according to Reuters.

Novartis (NVS) acquired MorphoSys for around $2.9B earlier this year.

data.title: Viking deserves same multiple as 'best in class' Royal Caribbean - analyst
data.description:
• Citi Research initiated coverage of Viking Holdings (NYSE:VIK) with a Buy rating and $54 price target, with compelling opportunities for the company as a “premier riverboat cruise operator” amplified by similar investment characteristics as its cruise peers.
  • “Viking has the best growth profile of the cruise companies, and while this is accompanied by a relative valuation premium, we believe that the group as a whole is undervalued,” said Citi analyst James Hardiman.
  • Hardiman believes Viking (VIK) deserves a comparable multiple to Royal Caribbean (RCL), viewed as the best-in-class among cruise operators.
  • Shares of Viking Holdings (VIK) are up 1%.

data.title: Western Digital downgraded to Hold following Micron guidance: Benchmark
data.description:
Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) was downgraded to Hold from Buy by Benchmark following weaker-than-expected second quarter fiscal 2025 guidance by competitor Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) on Wednesday.

Western Digital shares were down 4% near noon trading on Thursday.

"We see a growing number of concerns for Western Digital including softening NAND pricing driven by customer inventory adjustments in consumer markets, continued expected slowness in NAND chip demand in the industrial and automotive markets, weak F2Q25 guidance by Micron, and a declining share in the NAND market," said Benchmark analyst Mark Miller, in a Thursday investor note.

As a result, Benchmark decreased its fiscal year 2025 forecast on Western Digital for non-GAAP earnings per share to $7.57 from $8.16. They reduced the annual sales revenue forecast to $16.7B from $17.4B.

"Over the past three quarters, Western Digital's share of the NAND market has declined from a mid-teen percentage to 10.5%," Miller said. "We attribute this to the concentration of Western Digital's NAND sales to consumer markets which Micron does not expect to recover until later in 2025."

Western Digital remains in the process of spinning off its HDD and flash business units into separate entities, but it is unknown to the investing world when this will occur.

data.title: RayJay tweaks ratings on U.S. Bancorp, other banks ahead of 2025
data.description:
Raymond James collectively tweaked its rating recommendations on 11 banks within its coverage universe heading into the new year.

With President-elect Donald Trump set to take office in January, analyst Michael Rose favors banks that stand to gain the most from a deregulatory environment, as well as positive operating leverage and a steeper yield curve, he wrote in a note to clients.

He also noted that he prefers community and/or small-mid cap banks "that should benefit both fundamentally from an improving operating backdrop and use their premium currencies to deploy excess capital into smaller acquisitions where there are a relatively large number of opportunities to do so."

The rating changes include: Business First (NASDAQ:BFST), upgraded to Outperform from Market Perform; Commerce Bancshares (NASDAQ:CBSH), downgraded to Market Perform from Outperform, Hilltop Holdings (NYSE:HTH), downgraded to Underperform from Market Perform, Hancock Whitney (NASDAQ:HWC), upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform, Origin Bancorp (NYSE:OBK), upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform, OceanFirst (OCFCO, upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform, Old Second (OSBC), upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform, First Financial (NASDAQ:THFF), upgraded to Outperform from Market Perform, United Community (NYSE:UCB), upgraded to Outperform from Market Perform, and USCB Financial (NASDAQ:USCB), downgraded to Outperform from Strong Buy.

RayJay also upped U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) to Outperform from Market Perform, owing to its prospects for producing positive operating leverage in 2025 and 2026.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha's Quant system gives Northeast Community Bancorp (NECB) the highest rating among regional bank stocks, followed by Third Coast Bancshares (TCBX) and M&T Bank (MTB).
